name: Channel3 Commerce Product Search
description: Channel3-backed product catalog search. Accepts a text query and/or an image (image_url or base64_image) plus rich filters (brand_ids, gender, condition, age, price min/max, availability, website_ids, category_ids and their exclude_* counterparts) and a config block (language, country, currency, keyword_search_only). Returns compact product cards with merchant buy-offers (trychannel3.com affiliate links). $0.01 USDC flat per call.

usage.callShape: POST https://stableproduct.dev/api/channel3/search with JSON body {"query":"..."}; Content-Type: application/json. Pay $0.01 USDC via x402 (Base) or MPP. GET returns 405 (POST-only). Optional: limit (1-30, default 20), image_url/base64_image for visual search, filters{...}, config{language,country,currency,keyword_search_only}, page_token (use next_page_token to paginate), include_raw:true to also get Channel3's full upstream response under {data,raw}.
usage.outputShape: { products: [ { id, title, brands:[{id,name}], main_image_url, categories:[string], price:{price,compare_at_price,currency}, availability, best_offer:{url,domain,price:{price,compare_at_price,currency},availability,max_commission_rate}, offers:[{url,domain,price{...},availability,max_commission_rate}] } ], next_page_token }
quirks: POST-ONLY: a GET returns HTTP 405 (Allow: OPTIONS, POST). Registry historically lists GET — it is wrong.

RESPONSE IS AN OBJECT, NOT AN ARRAY: current endpoint returns {products:[...], next_page_token}. The prior 'top-level JSON ARRAY' quirk is WRONG for /api/channel3/search (it may only describe the legacy /v0/search path).
Body field is `query` (string). Empty {} is accepted/payable but useless; always send query and/or an image.
Visual search: pass exactly one of image_url (URI) or base64_image (there is also a dedicated /api/channel3/image-search route).
Flat $0.01 USDC on Base (eip155:8453 USDC); registry may advertise price as 'Varies' — it is fixed.
best_offer.url / offers[].url are buy.trychannel3.com affiliate buy-links; purchase happens OFF-platform, not via this endpoint (no checkout here).
Trimmed cards by default; pass include_raw:true to also receive Channel3's full upstream payload as {data,raw}. offer fields are limited to url, domain, price, availability, max_commission_rate.
Pagination via next_page_token -> page_token. limit max is 30.
filters use category/brand/website IDs (use the brands/categories/websites lookup routes to resolve slugs->IDs); do not pass display paths.
Two registered backends are the same op via current vs legacy path; v0 additionally accepts an optional `customer` object.
usage.guide: Channel3-backed product catalog search. Accepts a text query and/or an image (image_url or base64_image) plus rich filters (brand_ids, gender, condition, age, price min/max, availability, website_ids, category_ids and their exclude_* counterparts) and a config block (language, country, currency, keyword_search_only). Returns compact product cards with merchant buy-offers (trychannel3.com affiliate links). $0.01 USDC flat per call. POST https://stableproduct.dev/api/channel3/search with JSON body {"query":"..."}; Content-Type: application/json. Pay $0.01 USDC via x402 (Base) or MPP. GET returns 405 (POST-only). Optional: limit (1-30, default 20), image_url/base64_image for visual search, filters{...}, config{language,country,currency,keyword_search_only}, page_token (use next_page_token to paginate), include_raw:true to also get Channel3's full upstream response under {data,raw}. Output: { products: [ { id, title, brands:[{id,name}], main_image_url, categories:[string], price:{price,compare_at_price,currency}, availability, best_offer:{url,domain,price:{price,compare_at_price,currency},availability,max_commission_rate}, offers:[{url,domain,price{...},availability,max_commission_rate}] } ], next_page_token }

name: StableNinja Retail Products
description: Retail product data from major stores (Wayfair, Walmart) — product details by ID/SKU. Pay-per-call x402.

quirks: Input is via query string, NOT a JSON body. Required: product_id (Walmart item id, e.g. 10450114). Optional: domain (us|ca, default us).
The registry inputSchema is null; the real schema is only discoverable by base64-decoding the 402 payment-required response header (bazaar extension contains the queryParams JSON schema).
Price is $0.01 USDC on Base (eip155:8453) despite registry priceDisplay 'Varies'.
Endpoint also accepts HEAD/DELETE per schema but GET is the read call.
Use the Walmart us_item_id (the numeric id in a walmart.com/ip/.../<id> URL), not the SKU/UPC.
usage.guide: Call GET https://stableninja.dev/api/walmart/product-details with query params product_id (a Walmart numeric item id, e.g. the id at the end of a walmart.com/ip/... URL) and optional domain=us|ca. Pay via x402 (USDC on Base, ~$0.01). The product data is a plain synchronous JSON response at body.data — title, brand, price/price_display, availability, rating, review_count, images[], categories[], and specifications[]. body.status will be "OK" on success and body.parameters echoes your request. No auth/sign-in needed.

name: StableMerch Custom Merch
description: Create custom shirts/mugs from an image and ship them via Printify. Preview→prepare→commit flow: free SIWX draft + preview, then the single paid x402 commit produces a real shipped order (irreversible).

usage.callShape: GET /api/catalog (free) → POST /api/drafts (SIWX) → preview → prepare-order (SIWX) → commit (x402, ships goods)
quirks: GET /api/catalog and POST /api/drafts are FREE (SIWX); show preview_urls before anything else.
commit is the ONLY paid step and it SHIPS A PHYSICAL PRODUCT — irreversible. Requires explicit user OK + shipping address.
Never collect shipping/contact or call commit before the user approves the preview.

APPROVAL-GATED: this is an OUTWARD/irreversible action (real charge / order / shipment). Shape is from the provider OpenAPI/discovery and was NOT QA-executed (we do not place real bookings/purchases as tests). The needsApproval gate fires before any real execution.
usage.guide: StableMerch. 1) GET /api/catalog (free). 2) POST /api/drafts (SIWX) {client_request_id, product_slug, image_url, options} → preview_urls (render them). 3) POST /api/drafts/{id}/prepare-order (SIWX) {address_to} → order_ready. 4) POST /api/drafts/{id}/commit (x402) → ships. Only commit costs money and it's irreversible — defer to explicit user approval.

name: StableGiftCards Search
description: Search the Bitrefill catalog (1,500+ gift-card, top-up, and eSIM brands across 180+ countries) by name, country, type, or category. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged).

usage.callShape: POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/search with JSON body {query, country?, product_type?, category?, in_stock?, page?, per_page?} + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment). 'query' defaults to '*'. 'country' must be uppercase ISO Alpha-2 (US, IT, BR). 'product_type' enum: 'giftcard' | 'esim'. 'per_page' max 500 (default 25).
usage.outputShape: products[].{id, name, country_code, country_name, currency, categories[], recipient_type, image, description, termsAndConditions, in_stock, packages[].{id, value, price, amount}, range?.{min,max,step,price_rate}}, total
quirks: Auth is SIWX, NOT x402 — returns the SIWX challenge until signed; once signed it is FREE (no USDC). Use --siwx.
Use the product 'id' (e.g. 'netflix-usa') as product_id for /api/product and /api/buy.
packages[].id is product-prefixed with a '<&>' delimiter, e.g. 'netflix-usa<&>30'. For buying, /api/buy wants the bare packages[].value ('30'), NOT this id.
packages[].price is an internal minor unit (e.g. 48643 for a $30 card); packages[].amount is the real human value (30).
country must be UPPERCASE 2-letter ISO; lowercase or 3-letter is rejected.
Returns 'range' instead of fixed 'packages' for variable-amount products.
SIWX free read (no payment) — verified 2026-06-11.
usage.guide: Search the Bitrefill catalog (1,500+ gift-card, top-up, and eSIM brands across 180+ countries) by name, country, type, or category. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged). POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/search with JSON body {query, country?, product_type?, category?, in_stock?, page?, per_page?} + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment). 'query' defaults to '*'. 'country' must be uppercase ISO Alpha-2 (US, IT, BR). 'product_type' enum: 'giftcard' | 'esim'. 'per_page' max 500 (default 25). Output: products[].{id, name, country_code, country_name, currency, categories[], recipient_type, image, description, termsAndConditions, in_stock, packages[].{id, value, price, amount}, range?.{min,max,step,price_rate}}, total

name: StableGiftCards Product Details
description: Get full details and available denominations/packages for a single Bitrefill product by id. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged).

usage.callShape: POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/product with JSON body {product_id} (required, the 'id' from /api/search results) + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment).
usage.outputShape: {id, name, country_code, country_name, currency, categories[], recipient_type, image, description, termsAndConditions, in_stock, packages[].{id, value, price, amount}, range?.{min,max,step,price_rate}}
quirks: Auth is SIWX, NOT x402 — free once signed. Use --siwx.
Required field is snake_case product_id; use the 'id' from /api/search (e.g. 'netflix-usa').
packages[].value is the buy-time package_id (e.g. '30'); packages[].id ('netflix-usa<&>30') is NOT what /api/buy wants.
packages[].price is an internal minor unit; packages[].amount is the human value.
Variable-amount products return 'range' (min/max/step) instead of fixed 'packages'.
SIWX free read (no payment) — verified 2026-06-11.
usage.guide: Get full details and available denominations/packages for a single Bitrefill product by id. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged). POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/product with JSON body {product_id} (required, the 'id' from /api/search results) + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment). Output: {id, name, country_code, country_name, currency, categories[], recipient_type, image, description, termsAndConditions, in_stock, packages[].{id, value, price, amount}, range?.{min,max,step,price_rate}}

name: StableGiftCards Buy
description: Purchase gift cards, top-ups, or eSIMs from Bitrefill. PAID via x402 with a DYNAMIC price equal to the product cost in USDC (0-500 USD). On success returns invoice_id, tx_hash, amount_usdc, and orders with redemption codes (or status 'pending' to be polled via /api/invoice). OUTWARD/irreversible — actually spends real money buying a real product.

usage.callShape: POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/buy with JSON body {cart_items: [{product_id, package_id, quantity?, phone_number?, email?}]}. x402-protected: first call returns 402 with the USDC price; pay via x402 to complete. cart_items: 1-15 items. package_id is the packages[].value from /api/product (string or number). For phone top-ups include phone_number on the item.
usage.outputShape: {invoice_id, tx_hash, amount_usdc, orders[].{product_id, product_name, value, status, code?, pin?, link?}}
quirks: OUTWARD / IRREVERSIBLE: this buys a real gift card and charges the FULL product price in USDC (dynamic, up to $500), far beyond a flat x402 fee. NEVER auto-call. NOT exercised during remediation — documented from OpenAPI/guidance only.
Auth is x402 (the ONLY paid route on this origin); all other endpoints are free SIWX.
package_id = the bare packages[].value (e.g. '30'), NOT packages[].id ('netflix-usa<&>30').
Use a generous client timeout (60s+); response waits briefly for fast deliveries. DO NOT retry on client-side timeout — the x402 payment is already captured and retrying double-charges.
Orders may return status 'pending' if Bitrefill hasn't delivered; poll POST /api/invoice with the returned invoice_id to retrieve codes.
Max 15 cart items per order.

APPROVAL-GATED: this is an OUTWARD/irreversible action (real charge / order / shipment). Shape is from the provider OpenAPI/discovery and was NOT QA-executed (we do not place real bookings/purchases as tests). The needsApproval gate fires before any real execution.
BROKEN (MASTERKEY_ASSETS.md): 2026-06-08 charged $10 ($5×2) but Bitrefill returned 403 not_available, no code, no refund. Charge-then-fail — do NOT expose/retry.
usage.guide: Purchase gift cards, top-ups, or eSIMs from Bitrefill. PAID via x402 with a DYNAMIC price equal to the product cost in USDC (0-500 USD). On success returns invoice_id, tx_hash, amount_usdc, and orders with redemption codes (or status 'pending' to be polled via /api/invoice). OUTWARD/irreversible — actually spends real money buying a real product. POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/buy with JSON body {cart_items: [{product_id, package_id, quantity?, phone_number?, email?}]}. x402-protected: first call returns 402 with the USDC price; pay via x402 to complete. cart_items: 1-15 items. package_id is the packages[].value from /api/product (string or number). For phone top-ups include phone_number on the item. Output: {invoice_id, tx_hash, amount_usdc, orders[].{product_id, product_name, value, status, code?, pin?, link?}}

name: StableGiftCards Invoice Status
description: Check the status of a previously placed order and retrieve redemption codes/PINs/links once Bitrefill has delivered. Read-only poll for an invoice_id minted by /api/buy. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged).

usage.callShape: POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/invoice with JSON body {invoice_id} (required) + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment).
usage.outputShape: {invoice_id, status, payment_status, orders[].{product_id, product_name, value, status, code?, pin?, link?}}
quirks: Auth is SIWX, NOT x402 — free once signed. Use --siwx.
Required body field is snake_case invoice_id (string). {invoiceId:...} or empty body returns 400.

Unknown/invalid invoice_id returns HTTP 500 wrapping the upstream Bitrefill 404 ('Invoice not found', error_code not_found). A 500 here means a bad/nonexistent id, NOT a dead endpoint — verified live.

Read-only status/code-retrieval; it cannot be driven to a 200 standalone — you need a real invoice_id minted by POST /api/buy first. The 200-with-codes path was not produced in QA (no real invoice on hand).
This is NOT the purchase endpoint — /api/buy is. This only polls an already-placed order.
SIWX free read (no payment) — verified 2026-06-11.
usage.guide: Check the status of a previously placed order and retrieve redemption codes/PINs/links once Bitrefill has delivered. Read-only poll for an invoice_id minted by /api/buy. SIWX-gated and FREE (no USDC charged). POST https://stablegiftcards.dev/api/invoice with JSON body {invoice_id} (required) + SIWX wallet signature (no x402 payment). Output: {invoice_id, status, payment_status, orders[].{product_id, product_name, value, status, code?, pin?, link?}}

name: Laso Finance Order USA Prepaid Card
description: Create and fund a real US-issued USD prepaid debit card. The x402 payment EQUALS the card value: amount=5 charges 5 USDC on Base/Solana and loads a $5 card (min $5, max $1000). Returns a card_id with status='pending'; poll /get-card-data with the id_token to retrieve the card number/CVV/expiry once ready.

usage.callShape: GET https://laso.finance/get-card?amount=5&format=json — x402-paid (the charge = the amount). amount required (number 5-1000). format optional: 'json' (default, agent-friendly) or 'html' (browser redirect page). No Bearer token needed for this call — the x402 payment authenticates + identifies you, and the response itself returns fresh auth credentials.
usage.outputShape: auth.{id_token,refresh_token,expires_in}, callable_base_url, user_id, card.{card_id, usd_amount, country('US'), timestamp, timestamp_readable, status('pending')}
quirks: NOT CALLED (outward / irreversible): this provisions a REAL funded US debit card with REAL money. The x402 charge equals the card value, so amount=5 actually spends $5 (5000000 base units USDC), amount=1000 spends $1000. Requires deliberate human approval.
THE PRICE IS THE PRODUCT: there is no flat fee — the registry's $5 is the MINIMUM card value, not a service fee. Pricing is dynamic 5-1000 USD.
Pass &format=json so an agent gets JSON; default mimeType is text/html (browser redirect view).

Card returns status='pending' with a card_id (e.g. O-01KTN62...). The card number/CVV/expiry are NOT in this response — poll GET /get-card-data?card_id=<id> using the returned auth.id_token as Bearer token.
US-ONLY: USD card, US merchants only, physical goods must ship to a US address. For non-US use /order-intl-card instead.
Prepaid cards are not accepted at all merchants — check GET /search-merchants first.
Card + token are bound to the paying wallet (user_id); no separate SIWX/sign-in needed for this paid call.

VERIFIED via prior purchase 2026-06-08 (MASTERKEY_ASSETS.md): bought card_id O-01KTN62GBAKT1VERAFMC962NYN, $5 US balance. GET laso.finance/get-card?amount=N — the amount is the card VALUE loaded (recoverable), NOT a fee. Outward/irreversible (real money onto a real card) → needsApproval.
usage.guide: Create and fund a real US-issued USD prepaid debit card. The x402 payment EQUALS the card value: amount=5 charges 5 USDC on Base/Solana and loads a $5 card (min $5, max $1000). Returns a card_id with status='pending'; poll /get-card-data with the id_token to retrieve the card number/CVV/expiry once ready. GET https://laso.finance/get-card?amount=5&format=json — x402-paid (the charge = the amount). amount required (number 5-1000). format optional: 'json' (default, agent-friendly) or 'html' (browser redirect page). No Bearer token needed for this call — the x402 payment authenticates + identifies you, and the response itself returns fresh auth credentials. Output: auth.{id_token,refresh_token,expires_in}, callable_base_url, user_id, card.{card_id, usd_amount, country('US'), timestamp, timestamp_readable, status('pending')}

name: Laso Finance Order International Prepaid Card
description: Order a real international (non-US) prepaid debit card, admin-fulfilled within ~24h. The x402 payment is the on-card USD amount PLUS a 3.8% fee (min $100, max $1000 on-card). Returns a queued order; poll /get-card-data (card_type 'Non-Reloadable International') with the id_token until the card is fulfilled and details are ready.

usage.callShape: GET https://laso.finance/order-intl-card?amount=100 — x402-paid. amount required (number, on-card USD, min 100 max 1000); the actual charge = amount + 3.8% fee. No body. Like /get-card, the x402 payment authenticates you and the response returns fresh auth credentials.
usage.outputShape: auth.{id_token,refresh_token,expires_in}, callable_base_url, user_id, intl_card_order.{on_card_usd_amount, charged_usd_amount, status('queued'), timestamp}, message
quirks: NOT CALLED (outward / irreversible): provisions a REAL funded international debit card with REAL money. Charge = on-card amount + 3.8% fee, so amount=100 charges ~$103.80. Requires deliberate human approval.
MIN is $100 (NOT $5 like the US card) and max $1000 on-card. The registry's $5 price for this backend is WRONG — real minimum charge is ~$103.80.
OpenAPI marks requiresPayment:false but the description explicitly states an x402 payment of amount+3.8% is charged — treat it as PAID (the discover index lists it among paywalled routes).

Admin-fulfilled (NOT instant): order returns status='queued'. After fulfillment the card is REISSUED with a new card_id (issuer's tx id); the original card_id still resolves via queued_order_card_id in /get-card-data.
Poll GET /get-card-data?card_type=Non-Reloadable International (or by card_id) using the returned id_token; details appear when status='complete'.
A queued order can be cancelled via POST /cancel-intl-order before fulfillment.
Use /search-merchants?card_type=Non-Reloadable International to check acceptance.

APPROVAL-GATED: this is an OUTWARD/irreversible action (real charge / order / shipment). Shape is from the provider OpenAPI/discovery and was NOT QA-executed (we do not place real bookings/purchases as tests). The needsApproval gate fires before any real execution.
usage.guide: Order a real international (non-US) prepaid debit card, admin-fulfilled within ~24h. The x402 payment is the on-card USD amount PLUS a 3.8% fee (min $100, max $1000 on-card). Returns a queued order; poll /get-card-data (card_type 'Non-Reloadable International') with the id_token until the card is fulfilled and details are ready. GET https://laso.finance/order-intl-card?amount=100 — x402-paid. amount required (number, on-card USD, min 100 max 1000); the actual charge = amount + 3.8% fee. No body. Like /get-card, the x402 payment authenticates you and the response returns fresh auth credentials. Output: auth.{id_token,refresh_token,expires_in}, callable_base_url, user_id, intl_card_order.{on_card_usd_amount, charged_usd_amount, status('queued'), timestamp}, message

name: Laso Finance Auth
description: Obtain Laso Finance auth credentials by proving wallet ownership (SIWX / CAIP-122). Returns a 1h Firebase id_token used as a Bearer token for every other Laso endpoint, plus a refresh_token, the callable_base_url, and your user_id (lowercase wallet address). This is the required first step before any read (search-merchants, get-card-data) or before reading card details after ordering.

usage.callShape: GET https://laso.finance/auth — free, no x402 payment. Authenticated via SIWX: send a base64-encoded CAIP-122 SIGN-IN-WITH-X header proving wallet ownership (agentcash fetch handles this automatically). No query params, no body.
usage.outputShape: auth.id_token, auth.refresh_token, auth.expires_in (seconds, e.g. '3600'), callable_base_url, user_id
quirks: FREE / SIWX-only: no x402 payment, just a wallet signature (agentcash fetch supplies the SIGN-IN-WITH-X header).
auth.id_token is a 1h Firebase JWT. Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <id_token>` for ALL other Laso endpoints — they 401 'Missing or invalid Authorization header' without it.
agentcash fetch does NOT auto-attach the Bearer token to subsequent calls — you must capture id_token here and pass it manually in the Authorization header.
Renew with POST /auth (grant_type=refresh_token) using auth.refresh_token before the token expires.
user_id is your lowercase wallet address; all card/balance data is scoped to this identity.
The paid card-ordering endpoints (/get-card, /order-intl-card) ALSO return fresh auth credentials, so you never need to pay just to get a token.
usage.guide: Obtain Laso Finance auth credentials by proving wallet ownership (SIWX / CAIP-122). Returns a 1h Firebase id_token used as a Bearer token for every other Laso endpoint, plus a refresh_token, the callable_base_url, and your user_id (lowercase wallet address). This is the required first step before any read (search-merchants, get-card-data) or before reading card details after ordering. GET https://laso.finance/auth — free, no x402 payment. Authenticated via SIWX: send a base64-encoded CAIP-122 SIGN-IN-WITH-X header proving wallet ownership (agentcash fetch handles this automatically). No query params, no body. Output: auth.id_token, auth.refresh_token, auth.expires_in (seconds, e.g. '3600'), callable_base_url, user_id

name: Laso Finance Get Card Data
description: Retrieve details for the caller's Laso prepaid card(s). With a card_id, returns that single card including the card number, CVV, and expiry once status is 'ready' (US) / 'complete' (intl). Without card_id, lists all of the caller's cards of a given card_type. This is the polling endpoint to fetch the actual card credentials after ordering via /get-card or /order-intl-card.

usage.callShape: GET https://laso.finance/get-card-data?card_id=<id>&card_type=<...> — all params optional, no body. Requires header `Authorization: Bearer <id_token from /auth>`. Omit card_id to list all cards (returns {cards:[...]}); pass card_id to get one card (returns the single-card object).
usage.outputShape: List mode: cards[].{card_id, card_type, usd_amount, status, country?, card_details?{card_number,exp_month,exp_year,cvv,available_balance}, transactions[]}. Single mode (with card_id): same fields at top level, no `cards` wrapper.
quirks: FREE read, but REQUIRES `Authorization: Bearer <id_token>` from GET /auth first — 401 'Missing or invalid Authorization header' otherwise (agentcash fetch does not attach it automatically).
Response is polymorphic: NO card_id -> {cards:[...]} array; WITH card_id -> a single card object (no `cards` wrapper).

card_details (card_number/cvv/expiry/available_balance) is only present when status='ready' (US) or 'complete' (international); freshly ordered cards return status='pending'/'queued' with no details — poll until ready.
card_type filter (list mode) defaults to 'Non-Reloadable U.S.'; pass 'Non-Reloadable International' to see intl cards.

International cards carry extra fields (label, charged_usd_amount, fees_paid, state, queued_order_card_id) and a different transaction shape (merchant/status vs is_credit). After intl admin fulfillment the card is reissued with a new card_id; querying the ORIGINAL card_id still resolves via queued_order_card_id.
Empty result for a wallet with no cards is {cards:[]} (verified).
usage.guide: Retrieve details for the caller's Laso prepaid card(s). With a card_id, returns that single card including the card number, CVV, and expiry once status is 'ready' (US) / 'complete' (intl). Without card_id, lists all of the caller's cards of a given card_type. This is the polling endpoint to fetch the actual card credentials after ordering via /get-card or /order-intl-card. GET https://laso.finance/get-card-data?card_id=<id>&card_type=<...> — all params optional, no body. Requires header `Authorization: Bearer <id_token from /auth>`. Omit card_id to list all cards (returns {cards:[...]}); pass card_id to get one card (returns the single-card object). Output: List mode: cards[].{card_id, card_type, usd_amount, status, country?, card_details?{card_number,exp_month,exp_year,cvv,available_balance}, transactions[]}. Single mode (with card_id): same fields at top level, no `cards` wrapper.

name: Laso Finance Merchant Acceptance Search
description: Search Laso's crowdsourced merchant database to check whether a Laso prepaid card is accepted at a given merchant before relying on it. Returns matching merchants with an acceptance status (accepted / not_accepted / unknown), website URL, description, and usage notes.

usage.callShape: GET https://laso.finance/search-merchants?q=<merchant>&card_type=<Non-Reloadable U.S.|Non-Reloadable International> — query params in URL, no body. Requires header `Authorization: Bearer <id_token from /auth>`. card_type optional (defaults to 'Non-Reloadable U.S.').
usage.outputShape: merchants[].{name, url, status('accepted'|'not_accepted'|'unknown'), description, notes}, query, count, card_type, note
quirks: FREE read, but REQUIRES `Authorization: Bearer <id_token>` from GET /auth first — returns 401 'Missing or invalid Authorization header' otherwise (agentcash fetch does not attach it automatically).
Required query param `q` = merchant name (e.g. 'amazon', 'netflix').
card_type enum is exactly 'Non-Reloadable U.S.' or 'Non-Reloadable International' (note the trailing period and capitalization); defaults to 'Non-Reloadable U.S.'.
status='unknown' does NOT mean the card will fail — the DB only covers merchants users have previously tried (see the `note` field). Returns many fuzzy matches (37 for 'amazon').
Some merchants carry `notes` advising indirect use (e.g. load funds via Amazon Reload, then spend from the Amazon balance).
usage.guide: Search Laso's crowdsourced merchant database to check whether a Laso prepaid card is accepted at a given merchant before relying on it. Returns matching merchants with an acceptance status (accepted / not_accepted / unknown), website URL, description, and usage notes. GET https://laso.finance/search-merchants?q=<merchant>&card_type=<Non-Reloadable U.S.|Non-Reloadable International> — query params in URL, no body. Requires header `Authorization: Bearer <id_token from /auth>`. card_type optional (defaults to 'Non-Reloadable U.S.'). Output: merchants[].{name, url, status('accepted'|'not_accepted'|'unknown'), description, notes}, query, count, card_type, note

name: Purch Product Search
description: Search real, buyable Amazon/Shopify products by keyword (with price filters). Returns products with title/price/ASIN so you can then BUY + ship one via /x402/buy.

usage.outputShape: products[].{title, price, currency, asin, source(amazon|shopify), productUrl, imageUrl}, totalResults, page, hasMore
quirks: Solana USDC only ($0.01).
Keyword search; use /x402/shop for natural-language assistant ($0.10).
Capture asin (Amazon) or productUrl+variantId (Shopify) to feed /x402/buy.
usage.guide: Find a buyable product. GET with q (+ optional priceMin/priceMax). Pay ~$0.01 USDC on Solana. Take the asin/productUrl of the chosen item into purch-buy.

name: Purch AI Shopping Assistant
description: Natural-language shopping assistant: describe the gear you need (e.g. "universal travel adapter for Japan under $30") and get a curated set of buyable products back.

usage.callShape: POST https://api.purch.xyz/x402/shop  body {"message":"universal travel adapter for Japan under $30","context":{"priceRange":{"max":30}}}

inputExample.message: comfortable walking shoes for a week in Tokyo under $120

quirks: Solana USDC only ($0.10 — 10x a plain search).
Body requires `message`; optional `context.priceRange`/`preferences`.
Prefer purch-search ($0.01) when you already know the query.
usage.guide: Use when the user describes gear loosely. POST {message, context?}; pay ~$0.10 USDC on Solana. Pick a product and feed its asin/productUrl into purch-buy.

name: Purch Buy + Ship
description: BUY a real Amazon/Shopify product and ship it to an address — paid in USDC. The x402 charge EQUALS the product total (incl. tax/shipping). Outward/irreversible: real purchase.

usage.callShape: POST https://api.purch.xyz/x402/buy  body {asin OR productUrl (+variantId for Shopify), shippingAddress:{name,line1,line2?,city,state,postalCode,country(ISO-2),phone?}, email}
quirks: RED / needsApproval — this SPENDS REAL MONEY on a real good and ships it. Confirm the exact total + address + item with the user first.
The x402 402 quote (and the charge) EQUALS the product total incl. tax/shipping — not a flat fee.
shippingAddress + email are REQUIRED; Amazon needs asin OR productUrl; Shopify needs productUrl + variantId.
Solana USDC only. country is ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. US, JP).
usage.guide: Buy + ship a chosen product. Get the asin/productUrl from purch-search/shop, collect the shipping address + email from the user, show the confirm-gate summary (item + total + address), and only on explicit yes POST /x402/buy. The USDC charge equals the product total.
